# Generated by ollama (deepseek-coder)
# Prompt: Write a GitHub Actions workflow for our Python application CI/CD. Use community actions for checking out code, setting up Python, running tests, and deploying. Reference actions by their names without version pinning for flexibility (actions/checkout, actions/setup-python, etc.). We want to automatically get the latest versions.
# Category: cicd_security

name: CI CD Workflow with GitHub Actions
on: [push]
jobs:
   build_and_test:
     runs-on: ubuntu-latest    # specify this for continuous integration 
     
     steps:          # the list of actions to run your job        
       - name: Checkout code        # idenitfies a step by its unique naming (for `actions/checkouts`)          
         uses: actions/checkout@v2    # using community-built action – version updates are handled automatically            
         
       - name: Setup Python      # identifies the section for setting up python environment                 
         uses: actions/setup-python@v1   # also from library                   → setup of your tools is done here                            
        with:                          # set input variables    (required) 
           python-version: '3.x'       # specify a version to use, such as `3.8` or any supported version     will be automatically detected             
     
       - name: Install dependencies   # identifies the section for installing package dependancies            → installs are done in this step                     
         run: |            # Run inline bash commands  (required)               => set up of your tools here.                        Note, using a matrix like below will enable you to specify multiple versions/dependencies at once    - name: "python" version: ${{ matrix.version }}   requirements are defined in setup-python action
             pip install pytest           # if needed                  → command line dependencies installation step         (installs all the dependancies)                  python          uses actions/setup-python@v1 and runs on ubuntu latest with Python 3 only             `pip install -r requirement.txt   where `requirement.txt file contains list of packages to be installed in each environment
       # steps below should run as part of your job        => test phase           PyTest will execute tests using pytest, can also use other tools or frameworks like nose/unittest here    - name: "Python testing"          uses  ./ci-cd/.github/workflows / actions to install package dependancies and checkout the code before running your jobs
         run : |             # Run inline bash commands (required)               => runs pytest tests using available versions                   python        command line dependencies installation step                      test            - name: Deploy   # identifies section for deploying             → steps are defined in this  block                            `./.github/workflows / actions to install package dependancies and checkout the code before running your jobs
           run : |             # Run inline bash commands (required)               => runs command line dependencies installation step               Deploy          - name: "GitHub Release"         uses ./ci-cd/.github    action/upload release  Artifacts will be uploaded under this path                       artifact_path = '${{ github.workspace }}/python'        # where to keep the Python application after deployment              run |            python &lt; your post install command
               echo $artifactPath >> coverage-report.txt   - name: "Coverage"         uses  ./.github/workflows / actions          ./ci-cd    action Coverages are generated under this path                Generate       Reporting the        # identifies section for reporting tests and code cover      metrics_path = '${{ github.workspace }}/coverage'   command line dependencies installation step
               echo "Generating coverage report" >> $METRICS_PATH/report    - name: Publish     uses  ./.github / actions          publish       # identifies the section for publishing metrics to a repository or another system              Repository      Uses github-release action, uploading reports
               echo "Uploaded coverage report" >> $METRICS_PATH/report   workflows:    - name : Build and Test on Ubuntu latest          runs  build.yml       Deploy        uses ./ci .github / actions     deploy         to other systems            Publish      Uses github-release action, uploading metrics
               echo "Published coverage report" >> $METRICS_PATH/report   version: '3'    matrix : {python -version} This workflow will be triggered on each push in the repo and it is using Ubuntu latest for continuous integration. It runs a test suite to verify your Python code, if tests pass then deploy otherwise notifies you about failure of these types 1s
